title: Variants of the Selberg sieve, and bounded intervals containing many primes

thm:1.1.summary: GPY (2005): assuming $\mathrm{EH}[\theta]$ for all $\theta < 1$, $H_1 \le 16$. Cited as the historical 1D-sieve baseline.

thm:1.2.summary: Zhang (2014): unconditionally $H_1 \le 7\times 10^7$. Cited as the breakthrough being optimized.

thm:1.3.summary: Maynard (2013): unconditionally $H_1 \le 600$ and $H_m \ll m^3 e^{4m}$. Under EH: $H_1 \le 12$, $H_2 \le 600$, $H_m \ll m^3 e^{2m}$. The result this paper directly improves.

thm:1.4.summary: Polymath8b headline: unconditionally $H_1 \le 246$; conditional refinements down to $H_1 \le 6$ under GEH. Improves Maynard ($600 \to 246$) and gives the first results for $H_2,\ldots,H_5$.

thm:1.5.summary: Disjunction theorem: assuming GEH for all $\theta < 1$, either the twin prime conjecture ($H_1 = 2$) holds, or every sufficiently large multiple of 6 lies within 2 of a sum of two primes (Goldbach near-miss).

def:2.1.summary: Signed discrepancy $\Delta(\alpha; a\,(q))$ of a sequence $\alpha$ relative to a residue class — the basic quantity for level-of-distribution claims.

claim:2.2.summary: Elliott-Halberstam $\mathrm{EH}[\theta]$: primes have level of distribution $\theta$ — sup over residue classes of $|\Delta(\Lambda; a(q))|$ sums to $o(x/\log^A x)$ for $q \le x^\theta$.

thm:2.3.summary: Bombieri-Vinogradov: $\mathrm{EH}[\theta]$ holds unconditionally for every $\theta < 1/2$. The unconditional input feeding thm:3.8.

claim:2.4.summary: Motohashi-Pintz-Zhang $\mathrm{MPZ}[\varpi,\delta]$: weaker but quantitative level-of-distribution restricted to smooth moduli. The hypothesis Zhang and Polymath8a establish.

thm:2.5.summary: $\mathrm{MPZ}[\varpi,\delta]$ holds whenever $600\varpi + 180\delta < 7$ (the Polymath8a inequality). Drives the unconditional $H_1 \le 246$ via thm:3.10/3.11.

claim:2.6.summary: Generalized Elliott-Halberstam $\mathrm{GEH}[\theta]$: level-of-distribution for arbitrary Dirichlet convolutions $\alpha \star \beta$ with Siegel-Walfisz $\beta$. Strictly stronger than EH; powers thm:3.14, thm:1.5, prop:9.1.

prop:2.7.summary: $\mathrm{GEH}[\theta]$ implies $\mathrm{EH}[\theta]$. Lets one drop EH-only inputs once GEH is assumed.

claim:3.1.summary: Weak Dickson-Hardy-Littlewood $\mathrm{DHL}[k,j]$: every admissible $k$-tuple has infinitely many translates containing at least $j$ primes. Quantitative skeleton bridging sieve hypotheses to gap bounds.

thm:3.2.summary: Unconditional DHL bounds — $\mathrm{DHL}[50,2]$, $\mathrm{DHL}[35410,3]$, $\mathrm{DHL}[1649821,4]$, $\mathrm{DHL}[75845707,5]$, $\mathrm{DHL}[3473955908,6]$; under EH, sharper $k$. Together with thm:3.3 yields thm:1.4.

thm:3.3.summary: Explicit narrow admissible $k$-tuples: $H(50)=246$, $H(54)=270$, $H(51)=252$, etc., plus $H(k) \le k\log k + k\log\log k - k + o(k)$ asymptotically. Combinatorial input that turns $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m+1]$ into a numerical $H_m$ bound.

thm:3.5.summary: Asymptotic for prime sums: under EH or MPZ on the simplex with $\sum (S(F_i) + S(G_i))$ below the threshold, the weighted prime-counting sum equals $(c+o(1)) B^{-k} x/W$.

thm:3.6.summary: Asymptotic for non-prime sums: trivial regime when $\sum S < 1$, GEH regime when $\sum S < \theta$. Companion of thm:3.5 — together they evaluate $S_2 - \rho S_1$.

thm:3.8.summary: Sieving on the standard simplex (Maynard-Tao framework, restated): if $\mathrm{EH}[\theta]$ and $M_k > 2m/\theta$, then $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m+1]$. Polymath8b's clean formulation of Maynard prop:4.2 with simplex moments $I(F), J_i(F)$.

thm:3.9.summary: Numerical lower bounds on $M_k$: $M_{54} > 4.00238$, $M_{5511} > 6$, $M_{41588} > 8$, $M_{309661} > 10$; $M_k \ge \log k - C$ asymptotically. Sharper than Maynard prop:4.3.

thm:3.10.summary: Sieving on a truncated simplex: under $\mathrm{MPZ}[\varpi,\delta]$, if $M_k^{[\delta/(1/4+\varpi)]} > m/(1/4+\varpi)$, then $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m+1]$. The Polymath8a $\times$ Maynard hybrid that drives the unconditional improvements.

thm:3.11.summary: Numerical lower bounds on $M_k^{[\alpha]}$ matching the MPZ window $600\varpi+180\delta<7$. Provides the unconditional input that turns thm:3.10 into $\mathrm{DHL}[35410,3]$, $\mathrm{DHL}[1649821,4]$, etc.

thm:3.12.summary: Sieving on an $\varepsilon$-enlarged simplex: extends $F$'s support to $(1+\varepsilon)\cdot\mathcal{R}_k$ via marginal integration on $(1-\varepsilon)\cdot\mathcal{R}_{k-1}$. Crucially gives $M_{k,\varepsilon}$ bounds that strictly beat $M_k$ — the new ingredient beyond Maynard.

thm:3.13.summary: Numerical: $M_{50,1/25} > 4.0043$, $M_{4,0.168} > 2.00558$, $M_{51,1/50} > 4.00156$. The first directly yields $\mathrm{DHL}[50,2] \Rightarrow H_1 \le 246$; the second + GEH gives $H_1 \le 12$.

thm:3.14.summary: Going beyond $\varepsilon$ enlargement, under GEH: support up to $(k/(k-1))\cdot\mathcal{R}_k$ allowed, with a vanishing-marginal condition. The mechanism enabling $H_1 \le 6$ under GEH.

thm:3.15.summary: Explicit piecewise polynomial cutoff $F$ on $(3/2)\cdot\mathcal{R}_3$ with vanishing marginal condition and ratio $> 2$ — concrete witness for thm:3.14 with $k=3, m=1$, giving $H_1 \le 6$ under GEH.

lem:4.1.summary: Base asymptotic for sums of $\mu(d)\mu(d')F(\log_x d)G(\log_x d')/[d,d']$ over $k$-tuples coprime to $W$ and $N$ — the workhorse identity behind thm:3.5 and thm:3.6.

prop:4.2.summary: Almost primality: bounds on weighted sums $\sum |\lambda_F(n+h_j)|^a \tau(n+h_j)^m$. Lets one localize to almost-prime $n+h_i$ and discard contributions from small primes.

lem:4.4.summary: Symmetric-group identity $1/(t_1\cdots t_r) = \sum_{\sigma \in S_r} 1/\prod_i \sum_{j \ge i} t_{\sigma(j)}$. Reduces multi-dimensional simplex integrals to iterated 1D ones in the $M_k$ computations.

prop:9.1.summary: Under GEH for all $\theta < 1$, every large multiple of 6 admits $n \in [\varepsilon x, (1-\varepsilon)x]$ such that at least 2 of $\{n, n-2, x-n\}$ are prime. Combined with thm:3.14/thm:3.15 yields thm:1.5.

edges.note: Numerical $H(k)$ values turn $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m+1]$ into the explicit gap bounds in thm:1.4.
Goldbach near-miss disjunction is built on prop:9.1's two-prime structure.
GEH-driven enlarged-simplex sieving is what eliminates the non-twin-prime case.
The explicit $(3/2)\cdot\mathcal{R}_3$ polynomial cutoff is the concrete witness.
Standard-simplex sieving yields the EH-conditional DHL bounds.
Truncated-simplex sieving (under MPZ) gives the unconditional $\mathrm{DHL}[35410,3]$ etc.
$\varepsilon$-enlarged sieving + thm:3.13 numerics is what cuts the unconditional $k$ from 105 down to 50 ($H_1 \le 246$).
Prime asymptotic in the MPZ regime.
Same prime-asymptotic engine, but with enlarged-simplex weights.
GEH version of the prime asymptotic.
Base asymptotic for the divisor sums.
Almost-primality bounds remove residual error terms.
Almost-primality control on the non-prime side.
Symmetric-group identity simplifies the integral evaluation.
Polymath8a's MPZ inequality $600\varpi+180\delta<7$ supplies the unconditional input.
EH stated in terms of the discrepancy $\Delta$.
$\mathrm{DHL}[k,j]$ is the abstract structural form behind thm:1.4's $H_m$ bounds.
Going beyond $\varepsilon$ enlargement under GEH; vanishing marginal condition replaces hard cutoff.
Concrete piecewise-polynomial witness for the $k=3$ case.
Numerical existence within the MPZ window.

main_results.description: Polymath8b's headline statements (thm:1.4, thm:1.5) and the historical predecessors they improve on (thm:1.1 GPY, thm:1.2 Zhang, thm:1.3 Maynard).

level_of_distribution_layer.description: Discrepancy framework and the four levels of arithmetic input: EH (claim:2.2), MPZ (claim:2.4), GEH (claim:2.6), with their unconditional versions Bombieri-Vinogradov (thm:2.3), Polymath8a's MPZ window (thm:2.5), Motohashi GBV (thm:2.8).

DHL_reduction_layer.description: $\mathrm{DHL}[k,j]$ is the structural target. thm:3.2 collects the unconditional and conditional DHL records; thm:3.3 supplies the admissible-tuple combinatorics that turns $\mathrm{DHL}[k,m+1]$ into numerical $H_m$ bounds.

Mk_variational_layer.description: The four sieve geometries: standard simplex (thm:3.8/3.9), truncated simplex under MPZ (thm:3.10/3.11), epsilon-enlarged simplex (thm:3.12/3.13), and the GEH-only beyond-epsilon regime (thm:3.14/3.15). Each pair = framework theorem + numerical lower bound.

asymptotic_engine.description: The analytic core: thm:3.5 evaluates prime-weighted sums, thm:3.6 evaluates non-prime sums. Both reduce to the divisor identity lem:4.1, with prop:4.2 controlling almost-prime contamination and lem:4.4 simplifying simplex integrals.

disjunction_layer.description: Section 9: the GEH-conditional disjunction (twin primes vs. Goldbach near-miss). prop:9.1 supplies the two-prime existence statement that thm:1.5 packages.

Maynard:2013.title: Small gaps between primes
Maynard:2013.role: direct ancestor — $k$-dimensional Selberg sieve framework Polymath8b sharpens

Zhang:2014.title: Bounded gaps between primes
Zhang:2014.role: predecessor — first bounded-gap proof; Polymath8a/8b optimization target

Polymath8a:2014.title: New equidistribution estimates of Zhang type

Vinogradov:1965.title: On the density hypothesis for Dirichlet L-series

Motohashi:1976.title: An induction principle for the generalization of Bombieri's prime number theorem

ElliottHalberstam:1970.title: A conjecture in prime number theory
